title: puccinellia festuciformis subsp. intermedia
intro: Puccinellia festuciformis subsp. intermedia, also known as the saltmarsh grass or intermediate saltmarsh grass, is a subspecies of grass that is native to the coastal regions of Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The medicinal properties of this plant include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Puccinellia festuciformis subsp. intermedia include its ability to aid in respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. Puccinellia festuciformis subsp. intermedia can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory and skin conditions.
